commented_code:
+[project]
+name = "rustbpe"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "A very lightweight Rust library for training a GPT tokenizer"
+readme = "README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+
+# when uv/pip invoke maturin through the PEP 517 interface, it always builds in release mode by default.
+[build-system]
+requires = ["maturin>=1.9.6,<2.0"]
+build-backend = "maturin"
+

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+[project]
+name = "rustbpe"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "A very lightweight Rust library for training a GPT tokenizer"
+readme = "README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+
+# when uv/pip invoke maturin through the PEP 517 interface, it always builds in release mode by default.
+[build-system]
+requires = ["maturin>=1.9.6,<2.0"]
+build-backend = "maturin"
+
discussion_comments.comment_body:
You can add rebuilds through `uv sync` with the following:

```toml
[tool.uv]
cache-keys = [
    { file = "pyproject.toml" },
    { file = "src/**/*.rs" },
    { file = "Cargo.toml" },
    { file = "Cargo.lock" }
]
```

This makes uv check whether any rust source file has changed and rerun the maturin build on `uv sync` or `uv run`.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+[project]
+name = "rustbpe"
+version = "0.1.0"
+description = "A very lightweight Rust library for training a GPT tokenizer"
+readme = "README.md"
+requires-python = ">=3.10"
+
+# when uv/pip invoke maturin through the PEP 517 interface, it always builds in release mode by default.
+[build-system]
+requires = ["maturin>=1.9.6,<2.0"]
+build-backend = "maturin"
+

commented_code:
# target torch to cuda 12.8 or CPU
 [tool.uv.sources]
-torch = [  
-    { index = "pytorch-cpu", extra = "cpu" },  
-    { index = "pytorch-cu128", extra = "gpu" },  
+torch = [
+    { index = "pytorch-cpu", extra = "cpu" },
+    { index = "pytorch-cu128", extra = "gpu" },
 ]
+rustbpe = { workspace = true }
+
+[[tool.uv.index]]
+name = "pytorch-cpu"
+url = "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu"
+explicit = true
 
-[[tool.uv.index]]  
-name = "pytorch-cpu"  
-url = "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu"  
-explicit = true  
-  
-[[tool.uv.index]]  
-name = "pytorch-cu128"  
-url = "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128"  
+[[tool.uv.index]]
+name = "pytorch-cu128"
+url = "https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128"
 explicit = true
 
-[project.optional-dependencies]  
-cpu = [  
-    "torch>=2.8.0",  
-]  
-gpu = [  
-    "torch>=2.8.0",  
-]  
-  
-[tool.uv]  
-conflicts = [  
-    [  
-        { extra = "cpu" },  
-        { extra = "gpu" },  
-    ],  
-]  
\ No newline at end of file
+[project.optional-dependencies]
+cpu = [
+    "torch>=2.8.0",
+]
+gpu = [
+    "torch>=2.8.0",
+]
+
+[tool.uv]
+conflicts = [
+    [
+        { extra = "cpu" },
+        { extra = "gpu" },
+    ],
+]
+# rebuild if rustbpe is modified
+cache-keys = [{ file = "pyproject.toml" }, {file = "rustbpe/**"}]

discussion_comments.comment_body: This rebuilds the nanochat package (this `pyproject.toml`) on change, while we want to rebuild rustbpe.
